title: Hold shared state in processes, not globals
description: Model mutable state as a process addressed by messages or an ETS table, never the process dictionary or module attributes.
body: Use when several processes need shared or mutable state. Do not stash state in the process dictionary, abuse module attributes (compile-time constants) as variables, or reach for a global mutable singleton. Actions: own the state in a GenServer/Agent and mutate it via messages, or use an ETS table for high-read concurrent shared data with clear ownership. Validation signals include a test that concurrent updates are serialized correctly and no update is lost. Risk boundary: faking shared state with the process dictionary or globals breaks isolation and produces race conditions the BEAM's per-process model was meant to prevent; data that must survive a process crash belongs in ETS/DB, not in volatile process state alone.
